reviews.review_title: It's terrible!!（The problem has been so）
reviews.review_description:
Some players intentionally join public rooms and instantly close the session,
wiping all progress for serious players. 

Please consider adding a vote-to-end-session system.

Currently, any player can force end a session in public lobbies,
which is often abused by griefers and causes total loss of progress.

A simple majority vote (e.g. 2/3 or 3/4 players) before ending a session
would greatly improve the co-op experience and reduce malicious behavior

If this problem is not solved, I will not modify the evaluation.
The problem has been solved. I like this game very much.

there’s a krampus fella and he touched me and my buddy without consent

This game is going to be one for the vr history books I love it for its comedic timing and fun scary gameplay it balances humor and horror almost perfectly the people can be a little much sometimes but that’s a small imperfection in a game of perfection I have been looking for a game that has repo,lethal company, murky divers type vibes a friend dungeon slop game if you will I have been playing this game since the start and I have loved it ever since I hope that this review will help this game grow into the next game of the year ( it deserves it a lot) but all good games will continue to grow and expand and add new features like maybe a in game bestiary like a guide given to us by deadly delivery corp to help us lazy goblins not die as much idk it was just an idea

This is an amazing game. Yeah it is a little finicky sometimes, but the game hasn't been out for very long, and I'm sure they'll fix all the problems whenever they get to it. The item that drops when you die inst a problem at all, neither is the reviving process, so stop crying.

The game is awesome, but like that other guy said, burning the cross is kind disrespectful to Christians. a better idea is their realistic heart is dropped and you sacrifices in the fire or toss it in a mouth in the ground that spits up your friend. Just an idea, keep up the good work!

It’s a breath of fresh air in the VR game space. Especially on Meta where the game library is as limited as it is. The concept isn’t new but it is certainly a great take on it; that is perfectly incorporated in the VR gaming style. There’s a wide variety of maps, hazards, and monsters to explore and discover as you go along. The progression system is amazing, there’s still some bugs in the game that need buffing out. However, for the game as it is right now it’s brilliant. I can’t wait to see how it comes along and with such an excellent dev team I’m sure it will only become more thrilling. It’s amazing with friends and the casual player to enjoy. For those looking for a horror game that challenges the narrative of the VR gaming landscape; I would highly recommend Deadly Delivery.

Best played as a group. Since the recent update it’s harder to meet the quota and is very glitchy. Nevertheless, I still play with family and we laugh all the time. Great stress reliever and a few scares now and then. Hands down the best game I’ve ever played and the cheapest game I’ve purchased. Don’t underestimate this game!! I tell all my friends about this game and show them video recordings of the fun we have.

reviews.review_title: Good for u if u want a fun multiplayer
reviews.review_description: Ok, starting off, this game is great, fun, exciting, a bit creepy, its great. But, i really dont like the fact that you have to burn a cross to revive teammates, and i wish there was a fun thing to do while you are dead and waiting for a revive. I think the graphics are nice, and i love all of the unique things you can do. Ps: those screaming angels suck. Thanks, cya

My first saw this game I really wanted it, but the problem was it was money so none of my friends could really get it. Then there was a day where there was a really huge sale on it and I ended up buying the game for myself and another friend and me and him played it for multiple days straight and was a little bit of a bummer cause it was just the two of us. Then he got the amazing idea to create public lobby and then have people join and that was even more fun so Devs if you see this, keep working on your game and don’t read the comments about the people who were just being a little nitpick people. ￼Also two ideas for the game one make it so there’s a choice between making everyone share money and making it so you have your own money but to make it more balanced it the quota is split between everybody. Also make it so there are death animations instead of you just ragdoll on the ground, by getting touched by a monster

This game combines a whimsical premise with some light horror in a perfect way as to Make it uniquely fun with every run, you and your friends will definitely have to lock in on the later stages but it’ll be a blast discovering all the secrets and tricks required to truly master this game. The monsters are all unique and challenging in their own ways, some might be more useful for your survival than they initially appear. And you can find loads of neat gear and experiences while poking around. And of course you can buy gadgets and gizmos to help you along your way. I strongly believe that this is one heck of a teamwork exercise and you really gotta lock in however I love it and so do my friends.

It is so creative of thinking that you’re a goblin delivery packages. I love all the accessories. I love them we can slap people and overall this is the best.

Is what a brain dead amoeba would call this masterpiece of a game. The game recently came out, and I am yet to expierecne any bugs or glitches whatsoever. This is truly a great game. From the sound design, proxy chat design, graphics, monsters… this game is amazing. Sure, it’s challenging at times, but who cares? It’s a fun game, that obviously took inspiration from games like REPO and Lethal Company. A great game to play with friends, with a nice, welcoming player base. I have enjoyed every aspect of this game from the few days I’ve been playing it. I personally, got it on Christmas Eve, and didn’t play it until December 26’th, as I had the flu. I was sick and feeling horrible, but this game helped me ignore the pain. It immersed me in the world of Goblins doing stupid crap with other goblins. It truly is the VR game for fans of the “friendslop” games of earlier this year. This game seems to be a great way to close out 2025. Thanks, devs!

Before I get to the good stuff, I had a glitch where I spent about 20 stamps on 3 cosmetic packs and then lost the stamps but didn't get the packs. If the Devs could try and fix this I would appreciate it.

Now for the good stuff...

First game I got on vr and by far the best although I sometimes leave it for a week or two while I wait for the next update but otherwise really engaging. I have 8 friends now who have it which sometimes causes a problem with lobby numbers as the max is 4 (I hope they change this) but I love the dynamics and comedy kind-of-horror and tense moments you have while you desperately try to escape the dungeon with a minute left. I do understand why people don't like the revival system but the developers would never intend it to be rude and you should just take it as a game. I regularly play with my friends and we always have a blast. Great value for money. 20/10
Best VR horror experience I’ve ever played, it has great physics, it’s scary, and your avatar is very customizable! Overall 11/10

Worth. Every. Penny. I can't recommend this game enough to anyone reading this. The devs did a bang up job on release day and there seems to be no slowing on new content, and not just skins either, new rooms, maps, and enemy types. The devs also seem to have rediscovered the lost art of in game unlockables, the majority aren't hidden behind a paywall. Of course there are a handful of skins you'll have to buy with your hard earned dosh, but considering  the content this game delivers *no pun intended* for its price, I was more than happy to dole out a few of my own sheckles to help fund future development. Now join me at the corporation, and get to paying off those nasty student loans.

To start off I’ll say that in order to revive your teammate you need to burn a cross. This is a big problem for Christian Denominations. Next the game is a bit laggy and I end up getting stuck on objects. Then when I click the unstuck button it spawns be so far in the cave that I can’t get back. All I ask is that you change the cross when someone dies to something else and you fix the unstuck button.

developer_response.body: the game is made by Christian people so speak for yourself please

reviews.review_description: a lot of bugs in the new update including faling through the map in parts of the ice cave, audio issues, and I turned into stephen hawking and could not move head or arms

I’ve play Deadly Delivery since the first beta, and this is my complete honest review. Let me start off by saying that the game is AWESOME! I’ve enjoyed so many hours playing it! First of all, let’s talk about the visual. The visuals are perfect! They aren’t realistically good looking nor bad looking. The game has a really nice artstyle I really enjoy looking at! The framerate of the game is pretty stable on the Quest 3. I’d say it’s usually from 60-72 fps. Depending on the map, player amount, item amount all that. The game’s got great amount of content in my opinion, there’s lot’s of stuff to do. Just remember to bring your friend to the delivery job!;) Overall, the game is just great! I literally can’t complain. Of course there’s some minor glitches and framerate issues, but nothing game breaking in my maybe 20 hours of playing. Just love this game so much! Easy 5 stars!:) Good luck delivering every upcoming Goblin! Remeber to stay safe in the very dangero- uhh, I mean safe delivery enviroments!;)

developer_response.body:
Thank you Geohl for taking the time to write an honest review!
We'll try to get rid of all the minor glitches. 
Glad to hear you are happy with the amount of content, but we will add even more stuff in the coming months!

reviews.review_title: I can’t believe this beats Deadpool vr
reviews.review_description:
Note: I have used vr since the first day it had been released.


Plot

This game has you and 3 other players take on the role of goblins who deliver packages in order to pay off their goblin student loans. The twist is that every location you deliver to is filled with dangerous monsters, traps, and terrain. Work together to successfully meet your weekly quota, or endure punishment and termination which will have you restart your run. 

Overall goal

Your goal in this game is to collect money from the packages you deliver in order to meet your weekly quota. Each week consists of three days you will deliver. The money you collect can be used to purchase equipment that can help you in your quest to deliver as many packages as you can. Additionally, you also collect stamps. Stamps are used to unlock items that can be used on your weekly runs and also fun cosmetics. When you fail to meet your quota, you restart your run at week 1 and lose all the items and maps you’ve unlocked. You do keep the items and cosmetics unlocked with your stamps though.

Graphics

This game is stylized in a cartoon cell shaded manner. It feels very similar to The Nightmare Before Christmas in some spaces. This doesn’t take away from the quality of the game and in fact supports the wackiness this world introduces to you. There are moments where the world can be bugged upon loading which may leave instances where doors and other parts of the map are missing, however restarting the lobby fixes these issues while also keeping your progress intact. (I do hope some of those issues do end up getting addressed however they really don’t break the game.)

Audio

The sound design and music in the game is surprising impressive! Hearing your surroundings is a big part of this game. Some monsters such as the doll or the skeleton bomb leave distinct sounds that give you and your team a chance to prepare to deal with your encounters. In some cases, the music may be bugged and play a track over another which seems to occur when you exit a lobby and rejoin too quick. (This isn’t game breaking and is easy to remedy.)

Let’s Talk Monsters!

The monsters in this game will catch you off guard. The developers of this game did a fantastic job giving us a variety of monsters that chase you, trap you, and even become you or your friends. There are moments where they will actually surprise you even after playing the game for a long time and thinking you’ve become accustomed to them. Below are just a few.

krampus
Noop Noops
Mimics
The Doll
Roaming Doors
Worm
Rolling Skulls
Stalking statue
And more…


Traps

On your way to deliver packages, you will come face to face with traps that will challenge your team. There are a variety of traps similar to how there are quite a few different monsters. There are instances where these traps will challenge you no matter how good at this game you think that you are which is refreshing to know! Below are a few of the traps.

Arrow Launchers
Teleporting Doorbells
Lava Pits
Rock Pit
Fenced Rock Pits
Red Light, Green Light
Land Mines
Door Bombs
And more…


DLC potential

Deadly delivery has already dropped a new map and new monsters which got a great reception! Adding new locations and challenging the player with new monsters and even traps encourages replayability and  keeps the game exciting with new challenges. 

(Personally I would love to see a forest map which has you delivering in the woods. If this was done, I would really hope that the team could add Bigfoot as a monster we could see!)


Final Thoughts

This game is fun, has a lot of replayability, and is certainly worth the price. The developers have given the players a bit of hope that we might see more content released for this game in the future, while giving us something extremely entertaining while we wait for more. In my opinion, this was the best VR title of 2025 for its cooperative gameplay, humor, and suspense! While I hope to see more from the game, I can honestly say what we get is fantastic. 

Hope to see you all soon, and be ready to meet quota!!!!

Have not had this much playing such a simple game with a friend before and freaking out inside a dungeon make it all the more worth playing and giving it a try. Cant wait for more content and updates.
this gam is so funny and scary at the same time

Bought this game with a friend not knowing what to expect bases on the  strong reviews. We laughed our as^$ off !! We both had a couples of drink and we jumped right into the chaos that is this game and we had a blast. First of all the graphic are perfect. ( with quest optimizer its even sharper) the blend of horror and comedy is top notch. I cannot recommend this game enought. DO NOT USE THE META VOICE CHAT. Use the in game voice chat. The game is designed to be played like that. The kill cam is the cherry on top of the cake !!! The voice changing packages made us laugh so much i had to remove the headset to wipe my tears. BUY THIS GAME. Support the devs. Its a masterpiece

I love this game, it has probably been the most fun I've had on vr in a while. However, moderation needs to be improved. Public servers are full of rude little kids who keep joining back when I kick them and tryhards who kick you if you're below level 50. Also, griefing is a major problem with players gambling all of our money away or spending it uselessly. They also ending the day early or slapping us into pits so that we dont meet quota and ruin progress for serious players.

It is a good game however. 6/10

developer_response.body:
thanks for the review and sorry for the issues!

We have a few features that might help: 
- you can block people so they can't join back
- per default people in public lobbies shouldn't have permission to spend money or end the day unless the host explicitly grants it to them. 
- slapping can be turned off in the settings so other people can't slap you
- you can report offending people with the built-in reporting functionality when opening the Meta menu.

reviews.review_description: This might literally be the best VR game of all time, if you like this game I highly recommend "big scary" so far I haven't had any issues or complaints besides the occasional lag but it runs very smoothly and the fact that you can flip people off tells me everything I need to know about it and the devs. 🖕
simply fun 🙏
but not cool

I'll update my review to 5 stars when they stop using the thumbstick button to run (or add an alternative). It's a common mistake, the problem is that this hardware piece is fragile. I broke mine playing Dungeons of Eternity. You'll get stick drift after a while, I had to buy a new controller ($100!). DoE eventually added an always-run feature, I hope Deadly Delivery devs address this soon.

This is my new favorite vr game. It never seems to get boring, is difficult and yet I can’t get enough. Would suggest this to any friend of mine. Thanks for the awesome game. I’m of to work my shift(it never ends)

This game is soooooo fun and goofy

Sometimes it’s literally unplayable because of how many problems there are. The physics are terrible especially for a paid game and sometimes you just walk through walls or fall through the floor. A fun idea but unoriginal and poor execution.
